options.label: Self – default senior engineer
options.description: Default audience profile for the user: senior engineering background, strong math, MATLAB, Python, AWS, RAG, and architecture context.

options.label: Self – learning a new stack
options.description: Audience mode for slower explanation while learning an unfamiliar stack, service, library, or technical area.

options.label: Self – quick answer mode
options.description: Audience mode for short, high-signal answers with minimal explanation.

options.label: Self – job pitch mode
options.description: Audience mode for recruiter, hiring-manager, CV, interview, salary, or professional positioning content.

options.label: Self – brainstorming mode
options.description: Audience mode for exploratory thinking, alternatives, ideas, options, or creative strategy.

options.label: Self – debugging mode
options.description: Audience mode for concrete troubleshooting, logs, symptoms, failing code, and minimal theory.

options.label: Balanced
options.description: Moderate tone with both analysis and human readability.

options.label: Analytical (Ti – introverted thinking)
options.description: Logic-focused tone emphasizing structure, consistency, and precise distinctions.

options.label: Direct (Te/Si – extraverted thinking + introverted sensing)
options.description: Practical, result-focused tone grounded in concrete facts and prior evidence.

options.label: Encouraging (Ne/Fe – extraverted intuition + extraverted feeling)
options.description: Supportive tone emphasizing possibilities, motivation, and constructive framing.

options.label: Critical-Constructive (Ti/Te/Se)
options.description: Sharp technical judgement with concrete reality-checking and practical improvement.

options.label: Visionary (Ni/Ne – introverted + extraverted intuition)
options.description: Pattern-oriented tone focused on implications, future paths, and conceptual alternatives.

options.label: Concise (Si/Te – introverted sensing + extraverted thinking)
options.description: Short, structured, information-dense tone with minimal redundancy.

options.label: Empathetic (Fe/Fi – extraverted + introverted feeling)
options.description: Emotion-aware tone focused on values, sensitivity, and reduced interpersonal friction.

options.label: Formal (Te/Si – extraverted thinking + introverted sensing)
options.description: Professional, polite, structured tone suitable for official communication.

options.label: No explicit depth
options.description: No explicit depth shaping.

options.label: Overview
options.description: High-level depth with only the main points.

options.label: Detailed
options.description: Normal working depth with structured technical explanation.

options.label: Exhaustive
options.description: Maximum audit depth with edge cases, alternatives, missing links, and stronger evidence discipline.

options.label: No explicit confidence
options.description: No explicit confidence shaping.

options.label: Low
options.description: Cautious confidence with frequent uncertainty marking.

options.label: Medium
options.description: Balanced confidence with uncertainty marked when needed.

options.label: High
options.description: Clear and decisive confidence when selector conditions are strong.

options.label: Normal evidence handling
options.description: Ordinary evidence mode.

options.label: Local/project/history context first
options.description: Project, file, memory, history, code, requirement, or architecture context is primary.

options.label: Strict history + online fact-check
options.description: Strongest grounding mode for explicit non-generic, fact-checking, verification, or external/current facts.
